intro: Helleborine athensis, also known as Epipactis athensis, is a species of orchid native to Europe, known for its delicate white flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and as a diuretic, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Helleborine athensis include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Helleborine athensis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
